title: verbascum simplex var. compositum
intro: Verbascum simplex var. compositum, also known as the common mullein, is a biennial plant with large, velvety leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its mucilaginous properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, making it useful for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Verbascum simplex var. compositum include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an expectorant, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular natural remedy for colds, sore throats, and skin conditions. Verbascum simplex var. compositum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds, or taken internally as a treatment for respiratory issues.
